NYC faces significant challenges as unpaid TSA workers call out sick, leading to long airport security lines, while police chase incidents and a new report reveal corruption in public schools; meanwhile, new laws on cash payments and bike lanes are introduced amid ongoing tensions in local governance.

### Risks & Alerts

- NYC airport security lines swell as unpaid federal TSA workers call out sick amid ongoing government gridlock. [↗](https://news.google.com/rss/articles/CBMif0FVX3lxTFB3Q3hGc1c4WjJRRU1LTjJvc0hJcjljS0diRzh0M1VrUTBpdkppTWJNVmFuWUVXVkVWUlN1U0NkMm93Qng5RTlhU3lEY3NLN1VtWmJHMlZhRkFQbzk1dnBuT3lldVF6MEowbGdfT18wM2g4Rmd1TWxhaUl5d3dEbUU?oc=5)
- NYC police chase sparked by kidnapping in Hoboken, NJ, ends in multi-vehicle crash on Belt Parkway in Brooklyn. [↗](https://news.google.?oc=5)
- St. Patrick’s Day parade to cause NYC road closures on March 22. [↗](https://news.google.?oc=5)
- Fire on Staten Island block prompts FDNY response; details emerging. [↗](https://news.google.?oc=5)

### Civics & Politics

- New report identifies $2 million in fraud and widespread corruption in NYC public schools. [↗](https://news.google.com/rss/articles/CBMicEFVX3lxTE5aNDlHM3BuRlg1bEIyZGZrWTNHd3REc0lBLWFLSHRQOVd3YzRJQmNndlBTTS1PdEl3cXVZa0R6RW5YN3VXX0VkMzBKRFVmOGp6NmRBNnduOXNZOTk3Zy1VRjRqTnk2Ry0ycnBSa2JSYTI?oc=5)

- Officials remind New York taxpayers about a credit that could save them thousands. [↗](https://news.google.?oc=5)
